criteria.criterion: AGENTS.md tells contributors and agents to verify `git config user.name` and `git config user.email` and to sign off with that configured real identity.
.agents/rules/rebar.md and any derived guidance no longer instruct every actor to use Joe Oakhart's bot identity.

All matches of joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com or Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart are ONLY under infra/, .github/workflows/, tests/, or docs/experiments/.

citation.description: CONTRIBUTING.md:211 has 'configured git identity' on one line; AGENTS.md:162-163 has 'git config user.name' and 'git config user.email' and 'configured git identity' (split across a line break); .agents/rules/rebar.md:29-30 has 'your own configured git identity' (split across a line break). The substantive requirement — identity-neutral contract with git config commands named — is present in all three files. No file names Joe Oakhart as the required sign-off for general contributions.

criteria.criterion: CONTRIBUTING.md, canonical agent guidance, and generated/imported guidance describe one consistent DCO contract: all three name 'the committer's own configured git identity (git config user.name/user.email)' as the sign-off source, and none names a specific third party as required sign-off for general contributions.

A deterministic consistency check exists and fails the build on a reintroduced hardcoded personal sign-off: implemented as a make lint-integrated grep step (script or Makefile target) scanning the specified files and failing on a match.
